column35: Comments
Killmonger is a bit of a pushover and stood no chance against Red Guardian and Gamora. Wish I could recruit RG rare but it did not show up. The Vishanti was useless as usual in our games.

First match went better with yelana helping keep people from getting assassinated with her undercover abilities. Had better luck in card draw in game 1 as well. We thought captain carter would help with a little extra power to end quicker in game two but the card draw luck was horrible. Red Guardian and starlord t’challa were stud all stars!

column35: Very unlucky game. My idea was if cards were undercover or artifacts the scheme couldn’t snipe them. I never saw red guardian in the hq. Dr voodoo was koed before he could make it to my hand. I couldn’t get enough attack to hit anything. The only vp I got was Sharon Carter going undercover. (That was the only non grey made it into my hand)
Still really missing those sidekicks.  Need something to do when I only have 2 recruit.

Wow! This encounter was super well designed well done! the interaction with last stand and the tide is crazy.  I thought it was super frustrating getting 7 recruit when the rares were 8 cost, but we managed to get the win in the end with some big human torch damage.  I tried one for fun and I got gladiator hulk going really well, but in the end the tide was too much and i had 5 escapes for that run.
Barely scraped by round 1, got wrecked round two

I knew I wanted to use Gladiator Hulk, but wasn't sure who to pair with him, so I narrowed it down to two choices and did one of each.  The games were very similar, but I went with the one that had one more turn but 11 more points.

column32: I did not play a second attempt.

column35: First play was better, but I messed up the turn count somehow. Can be rough if the Twists come out right to give you a small city in the early game.

These games did not go well. Feast is probably the keyword I have the most trouble playing around. 
It didn't hit me too badly here, main problem was a very slow start due to the wounds spacing themselves out making my first 5 or 6 hands weaker.

I appreciate the idea of the changing city size and last stand, plus lots of heroes that can take advantage of wounds. So it was neat to see but just wasn't particularly fun to play. I squeaked by in the first game and got destroyed by some poorly timed Feast effects in the second. Once I could get Skaar and Sentry going, things worked well, but up until then it just a rough go of it.

column31: I did not play a second attempt.
column32: I did not play a second attempt.
column35: This setup is tricky because I spent a lot of time having to rescue Hope and keep her off the city so she wasn't abducted. It definitely didn't help that Mephisto caused lots of wound accumulation for some junky hands. I think what saves the game for me was that I got an all recruit hand (5 basic agents and a Thor: Surge of Power) which allowed me to recruit Domino's rare and gave me some very powerful turns. I can see how this one is easy to lose, I definitely kept drawing sceme twists right after rescuing Hope.
Tons of wounds flying around. Probably should have pulled a hero to help thin the deck but opted for Iron Fist for support.

We tried Daredevil and Gambit out in our first match, hoping to use the OP duo of Bishop and Iceman in our 2nd game. Unfortunately our 2nd game was a total disaster... So I'm reporting the 1st match. We did ok, with wounds messing us up more than I thought it would. But we only we had one escape, and eventually we took Mephisto down.
Going out with a whimper unfortunately. Several unavoidable early Twists and Strikes led to rough starts with both games. Had two decent decks by the end, but took too long to get there.

column31: I did not play a second attempt.
column32: I did not play a second attempt.
column35: Played solo, my wife wasn't interested. Tried to draw my way through lack of MK and KO but was slow. Being restricted to just two sets at this stage despite only losing in one previous round felt a little anticlimactic.

Mephisto got the best of me. Not enough thinning combined with his immediate wounds kept me down just enough in a game that went the distance. Saved Baby Hope, recruited a bunch of rares, but couldn’t take him down. One attack away from victory to hit him twice in the “MCU” sides last turn (also would’ve had him with one more turn). The “Comic” side just couldn’t do anything, as evidenced by the VP diff. The first attempt went so poorly, I didn’t even record it…

I don't know if it was because of the setup or my poor life choices, or just dumb luck, but that sucked.  It just never felt like I could catch a break in either game, and I lost on the sixth Twist both times.

I had the flu while playing this setup. I waited a few days in hope of getting better beofre the deadline but alas that was not the case.

Now onto the games. I forgot to note the points for Attempt 1, but it was too hard to stop the baby from escaping. Jean Grey rescued some bystanders, but the synergy between Thor, Domino and Wolverine and Bishop and Jean grey was too lousy. I was sure I needed to KO starters, hoping Bishop would help with that, but alas, it didn't happen. I was able to recruit 3 rares (Domino, Bishop and Wolverine), but due to bad luck didn't play them at all or more than once.

In Attempt 2 I though about getting Punisher in as well, he can KO 0-costs, which is good, and would synergize with Domino's Tech cards. Thor was always gonna be a lousy hero and his cards felt like wounds. I even made the mistake to get deck 2 a lot of thor cards, which did help absolutely nothing to defeat the city until I could buy Thor's rare, but by then it was too late already. In attempt 2 the KO-ing went even worse than in game 1. In game 1 I KO'd one wound with Bishop's effect. In game 2 KO-ing happened a lot, but just by healing normally. Loads of turns had 2 wounds per hand and it sucked hard. I never got to building my deck strong enough to even fight Mephisto or the villains that held the baby. Felt very unfun for me, I think it's because of Hand Ninjas being chosen, they are of course terrible Henchmen that don't really do enough to help. Also the core team of Domino, Wolverine and Thor was so weirdly antisynergistic that I can't help but feel it was a nasty choice. Leaving us with two options that needed to provide both KO and in my case also bystanders proved futile. The decks didn't get up to steam and were just barely reacting to escapes and other effects that gave wounds. Maybe I should have chosen tow KO heroes or just two very strong synergistic heroes. I don't believe in my mood affecting the Villain Deck, but it might have something to do with it too. I'm feeling lousy because I'm sick and it's hard to stay cheerful when you're physically feeling terrible. I think the setup was hard anyway and I probably chose the wrong heroes to have a chance. For me this is about the worst way to end the tournament, but all in all it was great fun, especially the earlier weeks. So, thanks Kylah and Bageltop Games for your behind the scenes efforts. And until the next Tournament!

This was easily one of the most unique games I've ever played! I was able to recruit 3 rare cards (Iceman, Jean Grey and Drax) for my left hand, and they just defeated Thanos in 2 turns. I drew all my cards with Iceman's and Angel's card draw abilities, played Jean's rare to gain a bunch of bystanders and attacks, then doubled the attacks with Drax's rare. I had 60 attack, that is easily the most I ever had in a single turn :D Also my first time rescuing all the bystanders... except for that one that was left in villain deck. I also got a lucky draw with the Reality gem artifact when I was able to put a master strike on the bottom of the villain deck, and that was the only one to show up. It makes me a little sad to have 2 escaped villains, could've been a "perfect" game, but still it will be one of my most memorable games ever. I'm happy I had Iceman and Jean both available for this game!
I can't tell them apart! Which head should I go for?

Both games were a long slog but we chose our second game as we had no escape and our score was a little better but a slower game.
Angle paired with Ant-Man rather well with the Moon Knight deck just going along with little effect on the game.

column31: I did not play a second attempt.
column32: I did not play a second attempt.
column35: Fun. Scheme got sort of bypassed by double artifacts. Still, had rough start, 4-2, 4-2 hands and therefore two early escapes. Had no time for second attempt.

In looking over the setup before starting I thought this was going to be close to impossible without a fair bit of luck. My first game went down to the wire before winning on a hail mary on the last turn and this was after having 3 Master Strikes in the first 4 turns to do no damage. I considered counting myself lucky and just posting the 1st game and moving on with my life. I decided to give it another try and while the villain deck wasn't as kind to me in the 2nd game, I got some much better combos going by landing the Ant-Man uncommon and the Angel Rare which let me tee of on Thanos a few times per turn.
One of the more fun games of Legendary I've played in a long time. I'll be replaying this setup with my friends soon.

Lost the first attempt on turn 15. Boo

A last turn victory on my second attempt. Lots of artifacts and a thin-ish deck let left hand really do very well.
By turn 18 right deck was the same as it started thanks to some well timed early schemes and twists. That hand did so badly. But did manage one hit on grimace, thanks to the soul gem, which I love. With all my soul… gem. 
Thanks for the fun tournament!

column32: I did not play a second attempt.
column35: Slow burning game this one..

column2: Star Wars Episode II: Attack of the Clone
column35: Solid game. Our first one was not good simply because we underestimated the scheme and the villain count. But we went into the second game much more prepared. We hit villains where we needed to to keep the balance of kree/skull count the same, to keep the twists moot. Got lucky when we got 3 twists in a row when the counts were already balanced. It was enough for us to focus our efforts to the mastermind and quickly ended the game. Very fun in having to make sure the villain counts were equal before we fought any villains

This was a good combination to play, we enjoyed it. We have submitted our 2nd attempt, hoping that our first won't end up with more points - this is the first time it wasn't as obvious for us which one to pick. Due to some early scheme twists we had 5 villains carried away (escape) which is a lot, but we won in 21 turns so we are hoping that still helps us out winning in a shorter amount of turns. So many good hero options to chose from as well, except for 2 GotG we had already used in this tournament. Thank you for running the tournament - we are much looking forward to the next one!

both games were wins, with this setup i decided to go with the one with a few extra turns but only 2 escapes, because it seems to me its very easy to get a lot of escapes in this setup.
had some fun synergies and combo's and hit some high nr's because of it.
I had such a close scoring comparison both games I hope I chose the right one. I enjoyed this set up, games were interesting.

column32: I did not play a second attempt.
column35:
Funny setup, I interpreted it as Pryor as Palpatine not caring who wiped out who and hiding behind the innocent Senator bystanders. 
6 of first 12 turns were master strikes or scheme twists - so while we got a lot of early wounds we had a lot of KO power in our heroes. Only had one scheme twist cause escapes so overall that felt lucky.
Super fun.

The epic Loki definitely made wound management and issue. We were very lucky to have three master strikes left in the villain deck. The characters interacted well with each other. Player two wasn’t able to rescue many bystanders for black widow to work the second game. Iron Man Really came into the rescue when I was drawing my whole deck every turn. Fun setup for core set.
Epic loki is not a fun mm to play specially on a time scheme
Having already used Rogue and Wolverine in the past really hurt me here, because they would have been perfect for this.
Really underestimated how quickly the wounds would come out. Happy with the choice of Hulk / Captain America.

Wasn't particularly looking forward to this setup, and they were pretty miserable games to slog through. The setup might have been more interesting with a larger hero pool, but I had very few options left being only Core and having already played similar setups in the tourney. Both attempts ended the game with only 1 wound in the wound deck I think. Absolutely brutal. In my second attempt, I hit my 4th Master Strike on turn 6 and 5th on turn 10! The flood of wounds meant several turns were spent just clearing them from my hands. The heroes that could KO wounds didn't want to show up to be recruited. Least enjoyable setup of the tourney and a bummer to end on.
N/A

column2: What's Red & Black & Dead All Over?
column35:
1st attempt went pretty well, had 1 escape but got things under control. Mr. Fantastic generating 7 attack for the Mastermind was helpful.

2nd attempt looked like it was going to be bad. Had 2 instances where 2 Scheme Twists chained together, giving me flashbacks to Advanced Solo Master Strikes :( But then I managed to get a Yelena dodge/undercover combo that generated (near) infinite attack and was able to clear a full city and attack 2 Masterminds a total of 5 times to win the game.

But by that time, there were 3 escapes, so I decided to submit the longer game with less escapes. Not as hard as I was expecting, and a pretty fun time!
Really liked this setup, plenty of KO's and Undercover shenanigans to keep the Rise effects in check.
First player's deck round at  Galactus Hungers
Early rampages were rough and the amount of attack changing to track is a bit insane but getting both decks to fire on all cylinders was great

both these game went terribly, kept forgetting to do the ascended Masterminds strikes on game 1, so decided not to count it, then got a scheme twist chain on game two and got slaughtered. realised when counting the deck at the end we had one extra scheme twist. :(. sometime the RNG gods just get you...

Took a little while in both attempts to get the decks going enough to hit Doom.  On attempt 2 Hawkeye's rare in the instinct deck went to town on getting bystanders, stack was nearly empty.  Lots of fun, nice to feel competent here at the end of the tournament.

Battling the epic version of Doom was a nice change of pace, as it now got me down to just two cards in a turn when we drew a couple of Consecutive Master Strikes.
